34.reason: [When the stack is empty, pop() returns 0, which is a valid integer. The calling code cannot distinguish between an actual popped value of 0 and an error condition. This leads to incorrect output when a pop on empty stack should print "error " but instead may print "0 ".]

The calling code cannot distinguish between an actual popped value of 0 and an error condition. 61.reason: [Because pop() returns 0 on error instead of a special indicator (like EOF), this line will print "0 " even when the stack is empty, instead of just "error ". The error message is printed inside pop(), but then 0 is also printed here, causing incorrect output like "error 0 " or just "0 " if error printing is missed in logic flow.]
